title: Portable Context Control Plane — PlanningWarning
description: Internal object schema for a single planning warning record. Planning warnings are machine-readable string-code objects placed in planningWarnings[] (global per-run), SelectionDecision.warnings[] (per-decision), TraceEntry.warningsEmitted[] (per-decision), conflictResolutionTrace[].warningsEmitted[] (per-conflict), and SelectionDecision.budgetWarningCodes[] (budget per-decision). Warning codes are typed as string with an advisory (open) enum of known codes — NOT a closed strict enum. This allows forward compatibility as new warning codes are added across passes. Canonical owner: docs/06 §3.4; docs/12 §6.2.
$comment: Pass 4.9C-3 (Batch C creation). No code. No runtime. No provider/model calls. Canonical: docs/06 §3.4; docs/12 §6.2. WARNING: warning code enum is ADVISORY OPEN — do NOT enforce it as a closed enum. Harness checks compare against exact string literals directly.

code.description: Machine-readable warning code. Must be a known coded string constant — no free-form prose. The enum below is ADVISORY and OPEN: new codes may be added across passes without a schema change. Harness checks must compare against exact string literals, not against a schema-enforced closed enum. Canonical: docs/06 §3.4; docs/12 §6.2.
code.$comment: Advisory enum of known warning codes as of Pass 4.9C-3 (corrected in Pass 4.9C-3R). This list is not exhaustive and must not be enforced as a closed enum by the schema validator. New codes added in future passes do not require a schema change — the 'string' type is the enforcement boundary. INTENTIONAL EXCLUSION: 'injection_suspect_seen' is NOT a warning code and is deliberately absent from this list — it is a trace/evidence atom that belongs in TraceEntry.evidence[] only. It must never appear in planningWarnings[] or warningsEmitted[]. Canonical: docs/06 §3.4; docs/12 §6.2.

componentId.description: Optional. The registry component ID associated with this warning, when the warning is specific to a particular component. Absent for global per-run warnings that are not tied to a specific component. Canonical: docs/12 §6.2.

message.description: Human-readable description of the warning. Must not contain raw component content, raw history turn content, raw user message text, secrets, credentials, or matched injection pattern text. Canonical: docs/12 §6.2.
